pr.title: Decompile some of ftlink and add an inline function for grabbing Fighter object instances from HSD_GObj pointers.
pr.author: ChrisNonyminus

summary: Decompiled an initial block of Link fighter code from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftlink.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftlink.c, added needed function prototypes, refined ftLinkAttributes to expose offset 0x28 as a float, and introduced an inline getFighter(HSD_GObj*) helper in fighter.h. The PR body explicitly says the inline was necessary for some functions to match.
what_changed: Removed roughly 305 lines of Link assembly and replaced them with C implementations in ftlink.c for functions around 0x800EAF38 through 0x800EB2F0.
Added C bodies for func_800EAF38, func_800EAF58, func_800EAFA4, func_800EB018, func_800EB060, func_800EB0A8, func_800EB11C, func_800EB1FC, func_800EB250, func_800EB2AC, and func_800EB2F0.
Kept later ftlink.s code but promoted callback labels to function symbols where referenced, including lbl_800EB334 to func_800EB334 and lbl_800EAF38 references to func_800EAF38.
Added inline Fighter* getFighter(HSD_GObj* fighterObj) in src/melee/ft/fighter.h returning fighterObj->user_data.
Updated include/functions.h prototypes for several callees used by the decompiled ftlink code, including func_800D94D8, func_800EB11C, func_800EC06C, func_800ECD04, func_800ECD58, and func_80149268.
Refined ftLinkAttributes in ftlink.h by splitting filler at 0x28 and declaring f32 x28, used by func_800EB250 when scaling Link attributes.
Added extern float declarations in ftlink.c for lbl_804D92D8, lbl_804D92DC, and lbl_804D92E0 with comments indicating 1.0f, 3.0f, and 0.0f.
smart_moves: Introduced a tiny inline accessor instead of repeatedly spelling fighterObj->user_data; the PR states this was needed for matching, and the new decompiled functions selectively use getFighter where it helps.
Recovered a dense branch ladder in func_800EB11C as a switch on func_8026B320(link->x1974_heldItem), preserving the observed case-to-argument mapping for func_80070FB4.
Modeled the attribute copy in func_800EB250 as a struct assignment between ftLinkAttributes pointers, matching the assembly copy loop at a higher level.
Exposed ftLinkAttributes::x28 as an f32 rather than leaving it inside filler, enabling readable C for the scale-dependent multiplication.
Converted assembly-only callback labels into global function names where other asm still takes their addresses, preserving symbol linkage while moving earlier code to C.
decomp_lessons: For Fighter objects, HSD_GObj->user_data access may need to be wrapped in an inline helper to reproduce codegen; use getFighter(HSD_GObj*) as a matching tactic rather than assuming direct field access always matches.
When a fixed-size assembly copy loop copies an ext_attr block into specialAttributes, try a full struct assignment if the struct layout is known enough.
Do not leave active fields buried in filler once code starts using them; here offset 0x28 in ftLinkAttributes became f32 x28 because func_800EB250 reads and scales it.
Simple assembly wrappers that only call another function can decompile cleanly as void functions, as shown by func_800EAF38 calling func_800EC06C.
When removing asm for functions whose addresses are still referenced by remaining asm, update label references to function symbols so address loads continue to assemble cleanly.
Boolean item-state checks around func_8026B2B4 and item-kind switches around func_8026B320 can often be represented directly as if/switch C without inventing semantic names before evidence exists.
naming_conventions: New inline helper uses camelCase getFighter and returns Fighter* from an HSD_GObj* argument named fighterObj.
Existing unknown functions retained address-based names such as func_800EB11C and func_800EB1FC rather than assigning speculative semantic names.
Link fighter local variables use the readable name link for Fighter* values derived from fighterObj.
ftLinkAttributes continues the project offset-field convention with names like x28 when the semantic meaning is not yet known.
Global float labels remain lbl_804D92D8, lbl_804D92DC, and lbl_804D92E0, with value comments rather than speculative constant names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use an inline getFighter(HSD_GObj*) accessor to influence matching for user_data loads.
Represent assembly compare-to-zero patterns from func_8026B2B4 as if (!func_8026B2B4(...)) when the branch structure matches.
Represent the multi-compare branch tree in func_800EB11C as a switch with cases 1 through 4.
Use struct assignment for the repeated paired-word copy in func_800EB250, then patch the scaled float field only when scale.y differs from 1.0f.
Keep address-taken routines globally named in assembly after nearby code is decompiled, as done for func_800EB334 and func_800EAF38.
